mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 7.5 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.5 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of housing starts and industrial production signals synchronized real-economy momentum. When both series rise simultaneously, corporate capex and consumer durable demand accelerate, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat when both proxies rise.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, rising CPI compresses real yields and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 90th percentile of its historical distribution, contradicting the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.5 percent over a 10-year backtest, with Rank IC consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which reduce input cost inflation and boost equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50 percent of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5 percent, invalidating the positive return signal.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion. This dual improvement reduces uncertainty and supports consumer-driven earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 40 percent of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market recovery phases, rising residential construction permits and mortgage-related demand indicators lead equity markets by pricing in future wealth effects and construction sector earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of housing recovery episodes, or the lead-lag correlation between housing indicators and SPX drops below 0.10 over a 5-year window.
